expectations.evidence: Missing standard ## header. Has Appetite but no Date/Status in standard format. Has Problem, Solution, Scope, Acceptance Criteria, Design Decisions, Failure Modes, Risks, Alternatives, Verification. But header format is non-standard — missing ## before 'Dashboard Annotations'. Has all content sections though. PASS on content presence despite header format.

expectations.text: no_code_fences: No code fences outside Implementation Notes
expectations.evidence: Zero triple-backtick code fences in the entire spec.

expectations.text: no_ts_interfaces: No TS types in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions contains no TypeScript syntax, interfaces, or type annotations.

expectations.text: no_json_shapes: No JSON/proto shapes in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: No JSON literals or data structure definitions in Design Decisions.

expectations.text: no_exact_file_paths_in_scope: Scope items are capabilities not file paths
expectations.evidence: In Scope lists behavioral capabilities (annotation proto type, CRUD API, visual overlay, context menu, annotations tab, URL query param, chat tools, chat context). No file paths.

expectations.text: bold_topic_sentences: Bold topic sentences in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: 6/6 paragraphs open with bold sentences: 'Annotations are stored per-dashboard...', 'The annotation proto type is a new top-level message...', 'Overlay rendering targets time-series-compatible panels only...', 'URL selection uses annotation ID...', 'Chat integration follows the established three-step pattern...', 'The Annotations tab uses existing hash-based tab navigation...'. 100% > 80%.

expectations.text: implementer_can_start: Implementer can start without questions
expectations.evidence: Names storage approach (separate resource by dashboard UID), API pattern, rendering target (TIMESERIES + HEATMAP), URL param strategy (annotation ID), chat integration pattern (three-step), tab navigation (existing hash). Clear enough to start.

expectations.text: no_implementation_leaks: Design Decisions are decisions not implementation
expectations.evidence: Design Decisions describes decisions at the architectural level — storage model, API design, rendering targets, URL strategy. Does not prescribe React hook internals or specific implementation mechanisms. 'useLatestRef pattern' in chat integration section is naming a known pattern, not prescribing implementation.

expectations.text: failure_modes_are_policy: Failure modes are policy decisions
expectations.evidence: 3/3 are policy: (1) creation fails → inline toast + preserve range for retry (could silently discard), (2) stale cache → show 'refresh' banner (could auto-retry or silently show stale), (3) shared link to deleted annotation → dismissible notice + default time range (could show error page). All debatable.
8/8 In Scope items are behavioral: proto type, CRUD API, visual overlay, context menu, annotations tab, URL param, chat tools, chat context. 100% > 80%.

## Acceptance Criteria exists with 7 checkbox items. All are observable outcomes: create from context menu → overlay renders, tab lists with filtering, URL param highlights, chat create with confirmation, chat list with filters, delete removes overlay, overlapping annotations visually distinct. None are implementation tasks.
### Agent-Verifiable subsection exists with 3 action→outcome checks: create via chat → appears in tab, URL with annotation ID → highlighted, delete → overlay disappears.
First content line: '**Appetite:** Full Cycle (~6 weeks)' with real time-budget value.

expectations.text: has_boundary_tiers: Three-tier boundaries in Design Decisions
expectations.evidence: Line 48: 'Always: render on TIMESERIES and HEATMAP panels', 'Ask First: any future panel type wanting annotation support must be explicitly opted in', 'Never: render on STAT, TABLE, LOG_STREAM, or ALERT_LIST'. 3 boundary-tier statements across all 3 tiers.
